com.mentor.chs.api.report

Interface IXDesignComparisonFactory


  • public interface IXDesignComparisonFactory
    Design Comparison factory is used for creation of the default svg builder, comparison builder, attribute comparator, properties comparator, connectivity comparator and object matcher. After creation of the comparators and matchers they have to be registered with the IXDesignComparisonContext to be able to use in the comparison. Changed object, change description and change type are the data structures that are used for implementation of a change on an object. These objects can be created using this factory class.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      IXChangeDescription getChangeDescription(java.lang.String description)
      This factory method allows to create change description with just desciption.
      IXChangeDescription getChangeDescription(java.lang.String before, java.lang.String after)
      This factory method allows to create change description with a value of the object in the reference(before) and current(after) design.
      IXChangeDescription getChangeDescription(java.lang.String descrption, java.lang.String before, java.lang.String after)
      This factory method allows to create change description with desciption and value of the object in the reference(before) and current(after) design.
      IXChangedObject getChangedObject(IXObject obj, IXChangeType changeType, IXChangeDescription d)
      This factory method creates an object of type IXChangedObject using the parameters provided.
      IXChangedObject getChangedObject(IXObject obj, IXChangeType changeType, IXChangeDescription d, java.lang.String objectDescription)
      This factory method creates an object of type IXChangedObject using the parameters provided.
      IXChangeType getChangeType(java.lang.String description, java.lang.String color)
      This factory method allows to mention desired type of change.
      IXComparisonBuilder getComparisonBuilder(IXDesign currentDesign, IXDesign refDesign)
      Returns the place holder for building comparison objects and matching objects.
      IXObjectComparator getDefaultAttributeComparator()
      The default attribute comparator that compares all the attributes except "Design", "NameOverridden", "DefaultName" and "AnalysisModel" on a pair of matched objects being compared.
      IXObjectComparator getDefaultConnectivityComparator()
      The default connectivity comparator that compares the connectivity on a pair of matched objects being compared.
      IXObjectMatcher getDefaultMatcher()
      The default matcher that decides corresponding pair of matching objects in the current and refernce design.
      IXObjectComparator getDefaultPropertiesComparator()
      The default property comparator that compares all the properties on a pair of matched objects being compared.
      IXSVGBuilder getSVGBuilder()
      Returns the SVG implementation for comparison.

    • Method Detail

      • getSVGBuilder

        IXSVGBuilder getSVGBuilder()
        Returns the SVG implementation for comparison.
        Returns:
        the svgBuilder upon which the changes can be annotated.

      • getComparisonBuilder

        IXComparisonBuilder getComparisonBuilder(IXDesign currentDesign,
                                         IXDesign refDesign)
        Returns the place holder for building comparison objects and matching objects.
        Parameters:
        currentDesign - is the design to be compared
        refDesign - is the design to be compared with
        Returns:
        the Comparison Builder that will be used to register the comparators and matchers.

      • getDefaultAttributeComparator

        IXObjectComparator getDefaultAttributeComparator()
        The default attribute comparator that compares all the attributes except "Design", "NameOverridden", "DefaultName" and "AnalysisModel" on a pair of matched objects being compared. IXAbstractPin is handled in a special way - no attributes are compared for these objects.
        To use this comparator register it with a comparison context IXDesignComparisonContext
        Returns:
        The default attribute comparator.

      • getDefaultPropertiesComparator

        IXObjectComparator getDefaultPropertiesComparator()
        The default property comparator that compares all the properties on a pair of matched objects being compared.
        To use this comparator register it with a comparison context IXDesignComparisonContext
        Returns:
        The default properties comparator.

      • getDefaultConnectivityComparator

        IXObjectComparator getDefaultConnectivityComparator()
        The default connectivity comparator that compares the connectivity on a pair of matched objects being compared.
        To use this comparator register it with a comparison context IXDesignComparisonContext
        Returns:
        The default connectivity comparator.

      • getDefaultMatcher

        IXObjectMatcher getDefaultMatcher()
        The default matcher that decides corresponding pair of matching objects in the current and refernce design. This is based on the base id and the objects being instances of the same shared object. In addition this will enable for matching objects based on name of the object.
        Returns:
        The default matcher. The name attribute is used to match similar objects, if the base uids are different.

      • getChangedObject

        IXChangedObject getChangedObject(IXObject obj,
                                 IXChangeType changeType,
                                 IXChangeDescription d,
                                 java.lang.String objectDescription)
        This factory method creates an object of type IXChangedObject using the parameters provided.
        Parameters:
        obj - the object to report on
        changeType - the type of change for this object
        d - Whats changed about this object
        objectDescription - Whats to display by default about this object
        Returns:
        an object of type IXChangedObject.

      • getChangedObject

        IXChangedObject getChangedObject(IXObject obj,
                                 IXChangeType changeType,
                                 IXChangeDescription d)
        This factory method creates an object of type IXChangedObject using the parameters provided.
        Parameters:
        obj - the object to report on
        changeType - the type of change for this object
        d - Whats changed about this object
        Returns:
        an object of type IXChangedObject.

      • getChangeType

        IXChangeType getChangeType(java.lang.String description,
                           java.lang.String color)
        This factory method allows to mention desired type of change.
        Parameters:
        description - the description of the change to appear in change column for example
        color - the color to highlight these changes on an SVG

      • getChangeDescription

        IXChangeDescription getChangeDescription(java.lang.String descrption,
                                         java.lang.String before,
                                         java.lang.String after)
        This factory method allows to create change description with desciption and value of the object in the reference(before) and current(after) design. For example a change in Name attribute of a Wire can be put in as getChangeDescription("Changed", "Wire1", "Wire1-1");
        Parameters:
        descrption - the decription of the change
        before - The value bofre the change
        after - The value after the change

      • getChangeDescription

        IXChangeDescription getChangeDescription(java.lang.String before,
                                         java.lang.String after)
        This factory method allows to create change description with a value of the object in the reference(before) and current(after) design. For example a change in Name attribute of a Wire can be put in as getChangeDescription("Wire1", "Wire1-1");
        Parameters:
        before - The value bofre the change
        after - The value after the change

      • getChangeDescription

        IXChangeDescription getChangeDescription(java.lang.String description)
        This factory method allows to create change description with just desciption. For example getChangeDescription("Property Changed");
        Parameters:
        description - the decription of the change
